entry.title: Inquiry on Depreciation Key Configuration to Comply with Vietnam Circular 45/2013/TT-BTC
entry.content:
Hi ALL,
We would like to seek your guidance regarding the depreciation calculation requirements under Vietnam Circular 45/2013/TT-BTC and whether SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud provides any standard depreciation key configuration or alternative approach to support this regulation.
According to Circular 45, the straight-line depreciation method is generally applied as follows:
Annual depreciation amount:
Annual Depreciation = Acquisition Value / Useful Life
Monthly depreciation amount:
Monthly Depreciation = Annual Depreciation / 12
In addition, for fixed assets that are acquired and put into use during a month, the first month's depreciation must be calculated proportionally based on the actual number of days the asset is in use:
First Month Depreciation = (Monthly Depreciation / Total Days in the Month) × Number of Days in Use
Where:
Number of Days in Use = Total Days in the Month − Asset Capitalization Date + 1
For example:

Acquisition value: 80,000,000 VND
Useful life: 10 years
Capitalization date: 11 July 2022
Annual depreciation: 8,000,000 VND
Monthly depreciation: 666,666 VND
First month depreciation (July): (666,666 / 31) × 22 = 473,117 VND

Under this regulation, depreciation is prorated based on the exact number of usage days in the acquisition month, while subsequent months are depreciated with the full monthly amount.
Could you please advise:

Is there any standard depreciation key in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that supports this depreciation method?
Can the period control method and depreciation key configuration be adjusted to calculate depreciation proportionally by actual days in the acquisition month while maintaining fixed monthly depreciation afterward?
If standard functionality does not support this requirement, are there any recommended alternatives, best practices, or enhancements available?

We would appreciate your guidance and any relevant SAP documentation or notes that address this requirement.
Thank you for your support.
Best regards,
Nghianv.

entry.title: Re: How to detect inactive DTPs in SAP BW Process Chains
entry.content:
SAP BW and SAP BW/4HANA already has a failsafe for inactive DTP-s in process chains. There is an automatic activation of inactive DTP-s when the DTP is triggered by a chain:

See CL_RSBK_DTP_API->EXECUTE in BW/4HANA and CL_RSBK_DTP->IF_RSPC_EXECUTE~EXECUTE in SAP BW:

* ---- activate DTP and (if necessary) transformation -----
        l_r_dtp->enqueue_dtp_for_request( ).
        l_r_dtp->set_activated_from_pc( rs_c_true ).
        l_r_dtp->activate_before_execute( ).

 

Of course if for whatever reason the DTP cannot be activated or if the DTP is not existing in A and M versions, then also the chain will not run.

entry.title: PTP WM Supplier return form - SAP S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
We are currently working on Supplier return Adobe form in S/4HANA Public Cloud, and we require Street, Postal code, Country field population in the Adobe Form.Since these fields are not available in the standard data form template(MMIM_GR4PO_COL_SLIP) , we implemented custom logic in BADI MMIM_ITEM_PROVIDE_DATA using CDS view I_Supplier based on supplier.Observation

  • The BAdI executes multiple times during the process.
  • During execution, the Street, Postal code, and Country values are correctly fetched and visible in the code tracing app.
  • However, the fields are missing in the XML and Adobe Form output.

    Clarification Required

    • Is there any recommended BAdI or enhancement approach to ensure data persistence for form output?

the generated solution is not working.

entry.title: Why we need to write more about our SAP Teaching Experiences
entry.content:
1. We Are Creative—but Are We Documenting Enough?

One challenge for teachers and researchers is simple: we are very busy.

We do many creative things in our classrooms, projects, and universities, but sometimes it is difficult to stop, look back, and systematize all the small pieces of information that make up an experience. Time is limited, and daily demands quickly take over.

I have always appreciated the model used at LAC 2024 and LAC 2025. We had the incentive to organize our experiences into short texts—and, for those of us who often need to write scientific articles, a short paper can feel like a blessing =)—reflect on them, and present them to the SAP University Alliances community.

Preparing a presentation forced us—in a very positive way—to transform individual activities, ideas, results, and lessons learned into a coherent story.

 

2. Deadlines Can Be a Good Motivation 😃

And, let's be honest: teachers and researchers seem, by nature, to be moved by deadlines. 😃

At LAC 2026, the model changed, and we did not have the same opportunity to present our teaching experiences. However, another opportunity appeared: the SAP Excellence Membership – Elevating Academic Partnership Through Recognition.

The SAP Excellence Membership recognizes outstanding lighthouse institutions in the SAP University Alliances network in the areas of Educational Excellence, Research Excellence, and Co-Innovation Excellence.

When the call opened, we saw it as an opportunity to look back.

Preparing our application required collecting many different pieces of information that was lost in cyberspace and computers: curriculum initiatives, teaching activities, events, innovative uses of SAP technologies, student learning experiences, and our engagement with SAP University Alliances offerings.

At first, these seemed like separate activities.

But when we started organizing and systematizing them, the pieces began to connect.

We could see a story.

 

3. Looking Back Helped Us See a Story (or sensemaking the journey)

Happily, this process of looking back and systematizing our experiences for the assessment contributed to UCS receiving the 2026 SAP University Alliances Educational Excellence Membership, recognizing our work with curriculum, academic activities, and innovative approaches to incorporating SAP technologies into teaching.

I am very grateful to Maria Calzada and the entire SAP University Alliances team for their support and for creating opportunities that encourage universities to reflect on and share their academic work.

To my surprise, our LinkedIn post about this recognition was also connected to the SAP Community through the post Recognizing Educational Excellence: UCS Honored at... - SAP Community

This was another small example of what can happen when we document and share our experiences.

One piece of content can connect to another. One experience can become part of a broader story.

 

4. A Coffee Conversation and a Simple Question

Another important reflection on the power of writing and systematizing came from a simple coffee conversation at LAC 2026. During the event, I had the opportunity to sit down for a coffee and chat with Katharina Schäfer, and one of the topics we discussed was exactly this: sharing our SAP experiences.

I talked about the LAC 2024 and 2025 model and how valuable it had been to have an incentive to organize and present our work. At one point, she asked me:

“Why don't you write more in our community blog posts?”

The question stayed with me.

I did not know the new community platform very well, but after exploring it, I found it quite interesting and easy to use.

More importantly, Katharina's question made me think about how many valuable experiences remain only in our classrooms, universities, presentations, computers, or personal files.

And perhaps we need to learn to write in a more agile way.

But first, we may need to change our mindset.

Perhaps we think an experience is too small to become an article.

Perhaps the project is not yet complete and we are afraid to talk about incomplete things.

Perhaps we are waiting until we have a “perfect” story to tell.

But maybe we should think differently—and more creatively.

After all, today we have many tools that can help us get started. AI can support the writing process. Voice-to-text can help us capture an idea after a class or an event. Notes, recordings, and other digital tools can help us transform scattered thoughts into a first draft. The technology is available. Maybe the bigger challenge is simply creating the habit of documenting our experiences.

 

5. Write Something. Share Something.

So, my invitation to the SAP University Alliances community, in this first article, is simple:

Write something. Share something.

I saw so many interesting cases at LAC 2026, and I am sure that in every university connected to SAP there is at least one valuable experience to share. That´s a lot of experiences given the number of universities and teachers evolved. These small pieces of experience, when connected through a community, can inspire new projects, encourage collaboration, and even promote new pedagogical models.

As I said before: professors are very creative.

So, let´s write about one teaching experience.

A classroom activity. A student project. An event. An experiment with SAP technology. Something that worked—or even something that did not work as expected. It does not need to be a large research paper or a long article.

Start small. Write something. Share something.

Over time, these small records can become a valuable collection of ideas, reflections, and lessons learned. They may inspire new projects, collaborations, research questions, or more structured academic publications.

Most importantly, they may help another teacher and the students. And most fundamentally:

Sharing and learning are essential to strengthening a digital community.

We already create many interesting experiences in the SAP University Alliances community. Perhaps we just need to write a little more about them.

So, what SAP teaching experience could you share today?

 

Disclaimer

GenAI was used to assist writing, but all the script, refinements, ideas and goodwill remain human 😃

entry.title: Re: Defect Duplicated
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Did you create defects from "Test Execution" app following steps: 
1. Click on 'Execute' of one test case
2. Click on '>' to navigate to the details of one test activity 
3. Click on '....' icon on the top right corner and select "create defect" button, a defect will be created.
4. After clicking on save or "save and close", one defect will be created and assigned. 
Please let me know if you follow the above steps and whether it works for you.

Best regards,
Ellie

entry.title: Program Teams in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
What Is a Program Team?

A program team is a group of people who work together across an entire program, not just one project. Think of a program as an umbrella that covers several related projects. A program team sits under that umbrella, so everyone who belongs to it is automatically visible and available across all the projects underneath.

This is different from a project team, which only applies to a single project. A program team saves you from adding the same people, with the same roles, to every project in the program one by one.

You will find program teams on the Program Detail page, in a section simply called Teams. The section title always shows the current count, for example "Teams (3)", so you know at a glance how many teams exist. Each team is shown as a row with its name and the list of members, so you never see the same person listed twice in one team.

 

Why This Makes Your Life Easier

If you have ever managed the same group of people across five different projects, you already know the pain: add someone to Project A, then remember to add them to Project B, then C, then realize you forgot D, and now someone is missing from a meeting invite because their name was never entered there. Program teams exist to make that problem go away.

Here is what actually changes for you:

  • Set it up once. Define your team at the program level, and it shows up in every project under that program automatically. No more re-entering the same names five times.
  • Everyone follows the same structure. Because teams are built from a shared set of roles, every project in the program looks and feels consistent, which makes onboarding new people, or just finding who owns what, much easier.
  • Nobody works off an outdated list. When membership lives in one place, you are not stuck wondering whether Project C has the current team while Project A is three names behind.
  • Changes stay under control. A program team is always edited from the program level, so there is one clear place to make updates, instead of five different places that might drift apart.
  • It keeps up when things move. If a project switches programs, its teams update on their own, so you are never staring at the wrong list.

Put simply, program teams take a job you used to do over and over, project by project, and let you do it once.

 

Getting Started: The Basics

Finding Program Teams

Go to the Program Detail page and look for the Teams section, just below the General Info section. If you do not see a Create button, it simply means your user does not have permission to create teams. Viewing and creating are two separate permissions, so it is normal for some users to see teams but not be able to add new ones.

 

Creating Your First Program Team

If you have the right permission, creating a team is straightforward:

  • Click Create in the Teams section toolbar. A creation screen opens beside your program details.
  • Enter a Name (required, up to 100 characters) and, optionally, a Description (up to 5000 characters).
  • Move on to add Team Members (covered next).
  • Click Save. You will see a confirmation message, and the new team appears immediately in the program's team list.

Two rules to keep in mind: the team name cannot be left empty, and it must be unique within the program (the system is not case-sensitive, so "Finance Team" and "finance team" count as the same name). If you break either rule, a clear error message tells you exactly what to fix.

 

Building Up Your Team : Assigning Members

When you build or edit a team, you choose how you want to work with members. Both views show the same data, just organized differently, and switching between them keeps everything in sync.

By Role View (the default)

Each row represents a predefined role from the program's role template. Roles that were custom-created are marked with a small person icon so you can tell them apart from standard roles.

  • Type a name into a role's input field to search for and select an existing user.
  • Type an email address instead if you want to invite someone who is not yet in the system. They will receive an invitation, and a message strip will list all pending invitations so you can track them.
  • Remove someone by deleting their token from the row.

 

By User View

Switch to this view using the segmented button if you prefer to think in terms of people rather than roles. Each row is one person, and the column next to them is a multi-select list where you assign one or more roles to that person.

  • Click Add to create a new row for another person.
  • Remove a row with the X button next to it.

A good habit at this stage: before switching between By Role and By User, make sure there are no unresolved errors (for example, an unmatched name or a missing role). The system will block the switch and show you exactly what needs fixing first.

 

Common Mistakes and How the System Helps You

The screen is designed to catch problems early, so you rarely lose work:

  • An empty team name or a duplicate name is rejected with a clear message before you can save.
  • A role input that is neither a matched user nor a valid email address is flagged as invalid input on that specific row.
  • An empty user row, a duplicate user, or a user with no role assigned in the By User view is each caught with its own specific message.
  • If you start typing changes and then try to navigate away without saving, a Discard Changes prompt appears so you never lose work by accident.

 

Getting More Advanced: Editing and Managing Teams

Editing an Existing Team

Open any team from the program's team list, then click Edit. The editing screen looks and behaves just like the creation screen: update the name or description, add or remove members, or change role assignments.

One feature worth knowing well is deactivating a role. Turning a role's switch off removes all its current members, so the system shows a warning dialog first, naming the role you are about to deactivate. If you are confident this is something you will do often, you can tick "Don't ask again" to skip the confirmation for the rest of your session. Cancelling the warning simply snaps the switch back on with nothing removed. Reactivating a role later is just as easy.

 

How Program Teams Show Up Inside Projects

This is where the real business value becomes visible day to day. Any project that belongs to a program will show that program's teams mixed into its own Teams tab, both in Project Overview and in Project Setup. They look like part of the project's team list, but they are clearly owned by the program.

From the project side, clicking on a program team opens the same team detail screen, but in read-only mode. There is no Edit or Delete button. Instead, an information strip explains that this is a program-level team and gives you a direct link to the program detail page, where you can make changes if you have the right permission.

This is a deliberate design choice: it keeps team management centralized at the program level, while still giving everyone visibility from wherever they are working.

One more thing worth knowing: if a project's program assignment changes (it moves to a different program, or is unassigned entirely), its Teams tab refreshes automatically. The old program's teams disappear and the new program's teams appear, so what you see always matches the project's current program.

 

Expert Moves

Turning a Project Team Into a Program Team

If you already built a strong team at the project level and want to reuse it across the whole program, you do not have to rebuild it from scratch. From a project's Crew Detail page, click Copy, and choose Program as the destination instead of Project. The existing project team is copied as a new program team, and you are taken straight to its detail page. This requires a specific copy permission, so if you do not see Program as a destination option, that is why.

 

Automating with the API

For customers and partners who want to manage program teams programmatically, SAP Cloud ALM exposes program teams through the same Projects API used for other project management objects. This opens the door to automation, integrations, and bulk operations beyond what the user interface offers.

The available endpoints cover the full lifecycle of a program team:

  • GET /programs/{programId}/teams retrieves all teams belonging to a given program.
  • POST /programs/{programId}/teams creates a new program team.
  • GET /programTeams/{teamId} retrieves a single program team by its ID.
  • GET /programTeams/{teamId}/roles retrieves all roles defined for a given program team.
  • PATCH /programTeams/{teamId}/programRoles/{roleId} replaces the members assigned to a specific role on a program team.

Together, these endpoints let you read a program's teams, create new ones, inspect their roles, and update role membership, all without opening the user interface. This is particularly useful for organizations that want to keep program team membership in sync with an external HR or resourcing system, or that need to set up teams in bulk when a new program is launched.

 

Deleting a Program Team

Deletion is only available from the program side, never from a project. Click Delete on the team detail page, confirm in the dialog that shows the team name, and the team is removed. The program's team list and its count update immediately. Cancelling the dialog leaves everything untouched.

 

Permissions at a Glance

Program teams are controlled by five separate permissions, so different people in your organization can be given exactly the level of access they need:

Action

Permission needed

View program teams from a project

hasProgramTeamDisplayAuth

Create a program team

hasProgramTeamCreateAuth

Edit a program team

hasProgramTeamEditAuth

Copy a project team to a program

hasProgramTeamCopyAuth

Delete a program team

hasProgramTeamDeleteAuth

 

Role Collections and Role Templates

Out of the box, SAP Cloud ALM ships one ready-to-use role collection called Program Administrator. Assigning it to a user gives that person the ability to manage and display programs and program teams in Project Management, without any extra setup.

Behind the scenes, this out-of-the-box role collection is built from a set of role templates that SAP provides for program-related access: Program Administrator, Program Manager, Program Team Manager, and Program Viewer. Each template covers a different level of access, from full management down to read-only viewing.

If Program Administrator does not exactly match how your organization wants to split responsibilities, you are not limited to it. Customers can build their own custom role collections by combining these role templates in whatever way fits their needs, for example a collection that only grants Program Viewer plus a couple of other unrelated permissions. The key point to remember: Program Administrator is the only program-related role collection that comes ready-made; anything else is assembled by your own administrators from the available role templates.

 

Quick Reference: The Business Rules

  • A team name must be unique within its program, regardless of capitalization.
  • Every user in the By User view needs at least one role assigned.
  • No duplicate users are allowed within the same team.
  • Inviting someone by email who is not yet in the system is supported; they receive an invitation.
  • Editing and deleting are only possible from the program side. From a project, program teams are always read-only.
  • If a project's program assignment changes, its list of visible program teams updates automatically.

 

Program Teams in Task Management

Program teams are not just something you set up and manage on the program side. They also become directly usable on individual tasks, right alongside project teams. This is where end users experience the real day-to-day value: staffing a task no longer means picking only from the project's own team.

Selecting a Team on a Task

Open any task and go to the Additional Information tab. You will find a Team field, and when you open its dropdown, the list is grouped into two sections: Project Teams and Program Teams. Both are shown together in the same dropdown, so you can pick whichever one actually has the person you need, without leaving the task.

Once you pick a team, for example a program team, the Assigned Role field right below it updates to match. If you chose a program team, the role list switches to show Program Roles (roles such as ABAP Expert, Change Manager, or Communications Lead defined at the program level) instead of the project's own role list. This keeps the role you assign consistent with the team you picked.

After the team and role are set, the Responsible field becomes a dropdown of the actual people who belong to that team. If your team is a program team, you will see the members of that program team, ready to select directly. The same logic applies to the Reviewer field: it also draws its list of people from whichever team, project or program, is currently selected on the task.

In short, once you assign a program team to a task, both Responsible and Reviewer can be filled with people from that program team, exactly the same way they would be filled from a project team.

 

Filtering Tasks by Team

The same project-and-program combination is available when you filter your task list, not just on individual tasks. Open Adapt Filters on the Tasks list, and you will find a Team filter field. Clicking it shows the same grouped list: Project Teams first, then Program Teams, with a (Not Assigned) option at the top for tasks that have no team set yet.

This means you can filter your entire task list by a program team just as easily as by a project team, for example to see every task currently assigned to a specific program-level team across a project. It is a quick way to get a cross-project view of workload for a program team, without having to check each project one by one.

 

 

Available Directly in List View and in the Spreadsheet

You do not need to open each task individually to make use of this. The same Team dropdown, with Project Teams and Program Teams grouped exactly as described above, is available inline in the Items list view. Click the Team cell on any row and the dropdown opens right there, with a search box at the top and a (Not Assigned) option, so you can assign a program team to a task without leaving the list.

The same capability now also extends to spreadsheet-style editing. When you export tasks to the spreadsheet view, both the Team and the Assigned Role columns support program teams and program roles, the same way they support project teams and project roles. This means you can bulk-assign program teams and program roles across many tasks at once in the spreadsheet, then upload the changes back, instead of updating tasks one by one.

 

 

Mass Edit Support

The same grouped Team dropdown, Project Teams and Program Teams together, is also available when you use Mass Edit to update several tasks at once. Select multiple tasks, open Mass Edit, and the Team field lets you choose a program team (or keep each task's existing value using the < Keep Values > option) exactly the same way as on a single task. This means a whole batch of tasks can be reassigned to a program team in one action, instead of updating them one at a time.

 

What This Means for You Day to Day

This is the payoff from everything above, in practice. Say someone on your program is only staffed at the program level and never sat on any single project team. Before, that person would have been invisible on tasks. Now you can make them Responsible or Reviewer just like anyone else, and their workload still shows up when you filter or track progress. You are not juggling two different systems for two kinds of people, project staff and program staff. It is one list, one way of assigning work, no matter where someone actually sits.

 

Summary : Get Started

Program teams let you set up who works on what once, at the program level, and have it show up the same way across every project underneath. You do not need to master everything on day one. Start small: open the Teams section, create your first team, and assign people by role. Once that feels natural, try the By User view, get comfortable with deactivating roles, and when you have a project team you like, copy it up to the whole program instead of rebuilding it.

The payoff is real: less retyping the same names into every project, fewer moments where one project has the right people and another does not, and one place you can trust for who is actually on which team.

entry.title: Assembly Subcontracting with Project Stock (Q) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Scope Item 6GD
entry.content:
Business Case: 

Scooter Manufacturer: Body Panel Painting 

ABC Scooter Company is executing a Government Fleet Project. The Body Panel is manufactured specifically for the project (Project Stock Q), while Primer and Paint are standard consumables issued from Regular Plant Stock. The subcontractor paints the body panel and returns the finished Painted Body Panel back into Project Stock (Q). 

By using Project Stock (Q) in S/4HANA Public Cloud, all materials and subcontracting costs are tied to the specific WBS element, ensuring strict project-level control, accurate budget tracking, and full traceability, critical for delivering complex projects on time and within budget. 

What changed in 2608:

  • The Subcontracting PO can now contain BOM with Components both from Regular inventory stock and Special stock (Project Stock: Q) in the same order.
  •  No stock type conversion required: Q transfers directly to R at the vendor.
  • Full ETO support: all costs tracked to the WBS element throughout GI, GR, and FI.
  • No financial document at Goods Issue: the FI/accounting posting occurs only at Goods Receipt.

For scope item 6GD, the stock type transitions are:

  • Project Stock component (e.g. RM0614): Q (at producer)  →  R – Subcontractor Project Stock (at vendor)  →  Consumed on GR
  • Regular Plant Stock component (e.g. RM0613): Blank ( ) (at producer)  →  O at vendor  →  Consumed on GR
  • Finished product (e.g. SG0612): Received back into Project Stock (Q) on GR
  • No financial or accounting document is generated when components are issued to the subcontractor (Goods Issue). This is a stock-to-stock transfer only — no consumption has occurred yet. The financial posting (cost to WBS element) is triggered only at Goods Receipt.

How to Set-up: 

Example Master Data: SG0612(main material), Components: RM0613 (regular or Plant Stock) and RM0614(Project Stock) 

  • In the material Master of RM0614: Go to MRP4 and set the individual/Collective requirement as 1(Individual) 
  • RM0613: Go to MRP4 and set the Individual/Collective Requirement as 2 (Collective) this allows the component to be planned pooled from plant stock.
  •  Note: The Individual/Collective Requirement setting on MRP4 is the most commonly missed configuration. RM0614 must be set to 1 (Individual) so MRP plans it per WBS element, not pooled. Without this, the project stock link will not be established correctly.

If the stock is insufficient, then Produce or Procure the components RM0613 and RM0614 according to your scenario. 

Step-by-Step Process Guide 

Step 1: Create Project Demand

  • Create a demand in the App: Manage Project Demand(F4562) by entering details as shown in the screenshot. 

Step 2: Run MRP

  • Either schedule MRP Run in the background or Run it as shown below: 
  • Go to the App: Monitor Material Coverage - Net and Individual Segments (Version 2)(F2101A)
  • Enter the Shortage definition as MRP Standard, Material as SG0612 (For which the demand is created in the above step) and then click Go. The details are listed. 

  • Select the demand and click Start MRP Run. 

  • After the MRP run, click Manage Materials. 

  • A Purchase Requisition should be created. 

Step 3: Create Subcontracting Purchase Order

  • Go to the App: Process Purchase Requisitions(F1048A).
  • Enter the Purchase Requisition or filter based on your criteria, select the Purchase Requisitions and assign the supplier, supplier should be determined based on the Purchase InfoRecords.
  • Then Click Create Purchase Order, The Purchase Order Simulation should be displayed.
  • Account Assignment = Q (Project/WBS) and Item Category = L (Subcontracting) must be pre-determined in the PO item table. Do not change these values.
  • In the Item Table, ensure that the account Assignment(Q) and Item Category = L (Subcontracting) pre-determined. Select the Item, in the Material Data tab, click on components. 

  • The Special Stock field must be set to Q for RM0614 whereas for RM0613 it should be blank 

  • Go to Account Assignment tab: WBS information should be determined. 

  • In the Confirmation Tab, select Inbound Delivery under the Confirmation Control. 
  • Save the Purchase Order and note down the number. 

Step 4: Post Goods Issue to Subcontractor

  • Go to the App:Subcontracting Cockpit (F2948) and enter the Purchase Order and click Go, the components details should be displayed. 

  • Select the components and click Expand all 

  • Then select all the components and click Create Delivery. 

  • Ensure all the details are determined, For example for RM0614, the Special indicator is Q, WBS information is as same as for which the demand was generated.  

  • Then click Create Delivery. An outbound delivery document should be created, note down the number. 
  • Login as Inventory Manager and check the Stock in the App: Analyze Stock in Date Range(F6185) before posting goods issue. 

  • Login as Shipping Specialist and go to theApp: Change Outbound Delivery(VL02N).
  • Enter the outbound delivery document, which was created in the previous step, go to picking tab and complete the picking process and save the document. 
  • Click Post Goods Issue 
  • Go to The Document flow of the Outbound Delivery and click on the material document which was created, check the below: for RM0613, once the stock moves to Subcontractor stock, it should be denoted with Special Stock type O (Subcontractor Stock). 

  • Whereas for RM0614: The Special stock indicator should be Q at the Producer and R once moved under subcontractor’s stock 

  •  Important: No financial/accounting document is generated at Goods Issue this is a stock-to-stock transfer only. No consumption has occurred yet. The FI/accounting document is generated only at Goods Receipt (Step 7) 

Step 5: Verify Stock at Subcontractor

  • Login as Inventory Manager and go to the App: Analyze Stock in Date Range(F6185) and check the stock details: 
  • You can as well check the stocks of each material in the App: Display stock overview(MMBE). And see the stock details, how many are with subcontracting vendor and how many with Producer. For example: 

Step 6 : Receive Inbound Delivery from Subcontractor

  • Login as Receiving Specialist and go to App: Change Inbound Delivery(VL32N) and enter the Inbound Delivery number and press ENTER
  • Select the Item and go to the tab: Stock Placement, Enter the Putaway Qty and click Save. 

Step 7: Post Goods Receipt

  • Note: The Goods Receipt is the trigger for the financial posting. The finished product SG0612 is received into Project Stock (Q) and the subcontracting fee + material costs are posted to the WBS element.
  • Go to the App:Post Goods Receipt for Inbound Delivery(F2502) and enter the Inbound delivery number which was created in the previous steps. 
  • Check the Special Stock type as Q and WBS information are predetermined. 

  • Select the Item and check the details. 
  • Check the component details are reflected rightly with respective special stock type for the component Material RM0613 and RM0614. 

  • Post the Goods Receipt and note down the material document. 

Step 8: Verify Material Document and Journal Entry

Doc Info tab → FI Documents → Journal Entry

  • Check the Material Document, following details should be available and Accounting documents should be generated. 

  • Click Doc Info tab and click FI Documents 

  • Check the Journal Entry: 

 Step 9: Create Supplier Invoice

  • Note: this is optional because we are focusing on the logistic process

Step 10 : Final Stock Verification

  • Check the Stock in the App: Analyze Stock in Date Range(F6185)

Summary:

Scope item 6GD closes a critical gap in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition by enabling full ETO subcontracting with Project Stock (Q). Manufacturers can now outsource operations while maintaining strict WBS-level cost control, complete material traceability, and automated financial posting all within a single integrated process.

Related Resources:

Assembly Subcontracting with Sales Order Stock (E) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: 7DW

Restrictions

entry.title: Maintain Authorization Group Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m looking for a solution in the Public Cloud to manage authorization groups. Here’s an example using Manage Characteristics (CT04).
As far as I know, in on-premises systems, this was done in Customizing, where you assigned authorization objects to the authorization group.
I haven’t found a matching option in the Public Cloud.

How is this done in the Public Cloud?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Why we need to write more about our SAP Teaching Experiences
entry.content:
Hi @Mateus_Panizzon 

Thank you for sharing this thoughtful reflection.

I particularly liked your point that many valuable SAP teaching experiences remain within classrooms, presentations, and personal files. The SAP Community becomes stronger when educators take the time to capture those experiences-whether they are success stories, lessons learned, innovative classroom activities, or even experiments that didn't go exactly as planned.

Your observation about "seeing a story" when looking back and connecting different initiatives resonates strongly. Often, what may seem like isolated activities can reveal a much larger impact when documented and shared with others.

I also appreciate your encouragement to "start small." Many community members may feel they need a perfect or complete story before writing, but some of the most useful posts begin with a simple classroom idea, student project, or reflection from an event.

Congratulations to UCS on receiving the 2026 SAP University Alliances Educational Excellence Membership, and thank you for encouraging others to contribute their experiences to the community.

I'm looking forward to seeing more stories from educators across the SAP University Alliances network. In the next months, a group of lectures from all over the world are going to be sharing very valuable content with this community. To stay tuned, I strongly recommend to join the group, subscribe to the relevant labels (either in the forum or blog boards), and check the upcoming events - and hopefully this post inspires many first-time authors to share something of their own.

As you said: "Write something. Share something. Sometimes a single post is all it takes to inspire a new idea at another university somewhere in the world."
 
Let me also connect you with @mattdyki who just shared his experience in a blog called "Teaching SAP without teaching SAP": Teaching SAP Without Teaching SAP - SAP Community

Best regards,

Isabel

entry.title: Connecting SAP Integration Suite to Azure Blob Storage / Azure File Share via Cloud Connector
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I would like to ask if anyone has experience connecting SAP Integration Suite to Azure Blob Storage or Azure File Share through SAP Cloud Connector.

I am aware that SAP Integration Suite provides dedicated adapters/connectivity options for Azure Storage services. However, in our case we are facing issues related to Azure regions/endpoints, and currently we are unable to use the standard adapter-based approach.

Therefore, I am investigating alternative options and would appreciate any guidance on the following:

  • Is it possible to access Azure Blob Storage or Azure File Share through SAP Cloud Connector?
  • Are there any recommended architectures or best practices for this scenario?
  • If direct connectivity is not supported, what alternative approaches would you recommend?

Our goal is to establish a secure connection between SAP Integration Suite and Azure Storage while avoiding the limitations we are currently encountering with the standard Azure integration approach.

Any suggestions, examples, or lessons learned would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: France: Register test participant ID with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition
entry.content: @Kasia4 No, we have created an OSS note for SAP. It seems to be a general problem and we are not able to proceed for now.. Let me know, if you find a way through 🙂

entry.title: Re: Teaching SAP Without Teaching SAP
entry.content:
Hi @mattdyki ,

Thank you for sharing this perspective. I really like the principle: "Teach concepts first. Use SAP to make those concepts visible."

Your examples show how SAP can help students connect theory with practice, understand real-world business challenges, and develop skills that go far beyond learning a specific software platform.

The story about replacing your proposal with a student's reflection was especially powerful-it perfectly illustrates where the real value lies.

Thanks for sparking this discussion. I'd also love to hear how others have used SAP to help students experience the concepts they teach.

Best regards,

Isabel

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA の SOAP API を使って仕訳伝票を転記してみた
entry.content:
本記事では、SAP S/4HANA の SOA Manager を使って SOAP API（Journal Entry Create Request Confirmation）を外部からコールできるよう設定し、実際に仕訳伝票を作成するまでの手順を解説します。RESTful API やOData APIが主流になりつつある昨今ですが、既存の EDI 連携や外部システムとのインターフェースには依然として SOAP/Web Service が多く使われています。SAP S/4HANA でも SOA Manager を使えば簡単に公開・利用できます。

 

1. 対象 API の概要

今回使用する API は以下のとおりです。

正式名称

Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous)

Technical Name

JournalEntryCreateRequestConfirmation_In

Namespace

http://sap.com/xi/SAPSCORE/SFIN

Operation

JournalEntryCreateRequestConfirmation_In

用途

外部システムから S/4HANA に仕訳伝票（Journal Entry）を同期的に登録する

このサービスの特徴として、1 回のリクエストで複数の JournalEntryCreateRequest を含めることができるBulk 対応になっています。大量データの投入には非同期版 API（Journal Entry - Post (Asynchronous)）の利用が推奨されています。

仕入先請求書を作成したい場合は、Supplier Invoice - OData V2 や Supplier Invoice - Create (A2X, Inbound, Synchronous) の利用を検討してください。

 

1.1 サービス構造（Service Structure）

リクエストメッセージは MessageHeader ノードと JournalEntryCreateRequest ノードで構成されます。JournalEntry ノードの配下には以下のノードが含まれます。

 

ノード

説明

JournalEntry

会社コード・転記日付など仕訳ヘッダー情報

Item

G/L 勘定科目の明細行

DebtorItem

得意先（売掛金）勘定の明細行

CreditorItem

仕入先（買掛金）勘定の明細行

ProductTaxItem

税勘定の明細行

 

なお、MessageHeader の TestDataIndicator を true に設定すると、実際に転記せずシミュレーション実行が可能です。動作確認時に活用できます。

スキーマや WSDL の詳細は以下の公式リソースで確認できます。

  • SAP API Business Hub - JournalEntryCreateRequestConfirmation_In: https://api.sap.com/api/OP_JOURNALENTRYCREATEREQUESTCONF_IN/overview
  • SAP Help Portal - Journal Entry Create Request Confirmation: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/f5c8d0579212c525e10000000a4450e5.html

 

2. 前提条件

  • SAP S/4HANA（本記事では S4H クライアント 400 を使用）
  • SOA Manager（トランザクション: SOAMANAGER）へのアクセス権
  • 仕訳伝票を作成できる FI 権限
  • SOAP クライアント（本記事では Postman または SAP Integration Suite を想定）

 

3. SOA Manager でサービスを公開する

SAP S/4HANA で SOAP API を外部公開するには、SOA Manager でサービス定義に対してバインディングを作成し、エンドポイント URL を払い出す必要があります。以下の手順で設定します。

 

3.1 SOAMANAGER トランザクションを起動する

SAP GUI でトランザクションコード：SOAMANAGER を入力して実行します。ブラウザが起動し、SOA Management の画面が表示されます。

図1: SOAMANAGER トランザクションの起動

 

3.2 Web Service Configuration を開く

SOA Management のトップ画面で、[Service Administration] タブにある「Web Service Configuration」をクリックします。

図2: Web Service Configuration の選択

 

3.3 サービス定義を検索する

Web Service Configuration の検索画面で、以下の条件でサービス定義を検索します。

Object Type

Service Definition

Object Name (contains)

JOURNALENTRYC*

 

「Search」ボタンをクリックすると、「JOURNALENTRYCREATEREQUEСТC...」というサービス定義が見つかります。

図3: JOURNALENTRYCREATEREQUESTCONFI のサービス定義を検索

 

3.4 サービス（バインディング）を作成する

サービス定義のリンクをクリックして詳細画面を開きます。「Configurations」タブを選択し、「Create Service」ボタンをクリックします。

図4: Create Service ボタンのクリック

 

3.5 サービス名とバインディング名を設定する（ステップ 1/4）

新規バインディング作成ウィザードが起動します。ステップ 1「Service and Binding Name」で以下を入力し、「Next」をクリックします。

Service Name

ZTT_sync_JournalEntryCreation

New Binding Name

ZTT_sync_JournalEntryCreation

 

図5: サービス名・バインディング名の設定

名前は任意ですが、後で識別しやすいよう API の用途を反映した名前にするとよいでしょう。

 

3.6 プロバイダーセキュリティを設定する（ステップ 2/4）

ステップ 2「Provider Security」でセキュリティ設定を行います。本記事はあくまで技術検証を目的としているため、設定をできるだけシンプルにしています。実環境では、以下の点を必ず見直してください。

Transport Level Security

None（HTTP）

Message Level Security

None

Authentication Method

User ID / Password（Basic 認証）

 

⚠️  実環境での設定について
本記事の設定は技術検証用です。実環境では以下を適切に設定してください。
・Transport Level Security: SSL（HTTPS）を使用し、通信を暗号化する
・Authentication Method: X.509 クライアント証明書や SAP Assertion Ticket（シングルサインオン）などのより強力な認証機能の利用を検討する
・Message Level Security: 機密性の高いデータを扱う場合は署名・暗号化を検討する
・接続元 IP の制限など、ネットワーク層でのアクセス制御も合わせて実施する

 

図6: Provider Security の設定（Transport Level）

 

図7: Provider Security の設定（Authentication Method）

 

3.7 SOAP Protocol を設定する（ステップ 3/4）

ステップ 3「SOAP Protocol」では Transport Binding の設定を確認します。「Calculated Protocol」が HTTP になっていることを確認し、「Next」をクリックします。特別な要件がない限りデフォルト値のままで問題ありません。

図8: SOAP Protocol の設定

 

3.8 Operation Settings を確認して完了する（ステップ 4/4）

ステップ 4「Operation Settings」では、このバインディングで公開するオペレーションが「JournalEntryCreateRequestConfirmation_In」になっていることを確認します。「Finish」ボタンをクリックして設定を完了します。

図9: Operation Settings の確認

 

3.9 サービスが作成されたことを確認する

ウィザードが完了すると、Configurations タブにサービス「ZTT_SYNC_JOURNALENTRYCREATION」とバインディング「ZTT_sync_JournalEntryCreation」が表示されます。State が「Active」になっていれば成功です。

図10: サービス作成完了の確認

 

4. WSDL と SOAP エンドポイント URL を確認する

サービスが作成されたら、SOAP クライアントから接続するために WSDL URL と SOAP エンドポイント URL を取得します。

 

4.1 WSDL URL の取得

作成したバインディングの Actions アイコンから「WSDL Generation」を選択します。「WSDL Flavours」でオプションを選択（デフォルトで問題なし）すると、下部に「WSDL URL for Binding」が表示されます。

図11: WSDL URL の確認

WSDL URL をブラウザで開くと XML 定義が確認できます。Postman や SoapUI にこの URL を貼り付けることでオペレーションが自動的にインポートされます。

 

4.2 SOAP エンドポイント URL の確認

WSDL 内の <wsdl:service> 要素にある location 属性が SOAP エンドポイント URL です。ブラウザで WSDL URL を開き、location を検索するか、以下のパターンで URL が構成されています。

http://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/srt/xip/sap/journalentrycreaterequestconfi/400/ztt_sync_journalentrycreation/ztt_sync_journalentrycreation

図12: WSDL 内の SOAP エンドポイント URL（location 属性）

 

5. SOAP リクエストを送信する

取得した WSDL URL を Postman や SoapUI にインポートし、SOAP リクエストを作成・送信します。リクエスト送信時には HTTP ヘッダーに SOAPAction を指定します。なお、リクエスト内の G/L 勘定科目・会社コード・仕入先 ID はあらかじめ S/4HANA に登録済みであることが前提です。

【HTTP ヘッダー】

Content-Type: text/xml; charset=utf-8
SOAPAction: http://sap.com/xi/SAPSCORE/SFIN/JournalEntryCreateRequestConfirmation_In/JournalEntryCreateRequestConfirmation_InRequest
sap-client: 400

【リクエスト Body（税コードあり・仕入先CreditorItemで締める例）】

<soapenv:Envelope
  xmlns:soapenv="http://schemas.xmlsoap.org/soap/envelope/"
  xmlns:sfin="http://sap.com/xi/SAPSCORE/SFIN">
  <soapenv:Header/>
  <soapenv:Body>
    <sfin:JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest>

      <!-- Bulk ヘッダー：複数の JournalEntryCreateRequest をまとめる単位 -->
      <MessageHeader>
        <ID>TEST-BULK-001</ID>
        <CreationDateTime>2026-07-10T10:00:00Z</CreationDateTime>
      </MessageHeader>

      <!-- 1件目の仕訳リクエスト -->
      <JournalEntryCreateRequest>
        <MessageHeader>
          <ID>TEST-001</ID>
          <CreationDateTime>2026-07-10T10:00:00Z</CreationDateTime>
        </MessageHeader>
        <JournalEntry>
          <BusinessTransactionType>RFBU</BusinessTransactionType>
          <AccountingDocumentType>SA</AccountingDocumentType>
          <CompanyCode>ZIT1</CompanyCode>
          <DocumentDate>2026-07-10</DocumentDate>
          <PostingDate>2026-07-10</PostingDate>
          <DocumentHeaderText>SOAP API テスト転記</DocumentHeaderText>

          <!-- 借方: 費用 G/L（税抜金額） -->
          <Item>
            <GLAccount listID="INT">66999981</GLAccount>
            <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="JPY">5000</AmountInTransactionCurrency>
            <DebitCreditCode>S</DebitCreditCode>
            <Tax><TaxCode>V3</TaxCode></Tax>
            <AccountAssignment>
              <CostCenter>ZIT1_CC01</CostCenter>
              <ProfitCenter>YB900</ProfitCenter>
            </AccountAssignment>
          </Item>

          <!-- 消費税明細（ProductTaxItem） -->
          <ProductTaxItem>
            <TaxCode>V3</TaxCode>
            <ConditionType>MWVR</ConditionType>
            <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="JPY">500</AmountInTransactionCurrency>
            <TaxBaseAmountInTransCrcy currencyCode="JPY">5000</TaxBaseAmountInTransCrcy>
          </ProductTaxItem>

          <!-- 貸方: 仕入先勘定（税込合計 = 税抜 + 消費税） -->
          <CreditorItem>
            <ReferenceDocumentItem>1</ReferenceDocumentItem>
            <Creditor>ZIT_BP03</Creditor>
            <AmountInTransactionCurrency currencyCode="JPY">-5500</AmountInTransactionCurrency>
            <DebitCreditCode>H</DebitCreditCode>
          </CreditorItem>

        </JournalEntry>
      </JournalEntryCreateRequest>

    </sfin:JournalEntryBulkCreateRequest>
  </soapenv:Body>
</soapenv:Envelope>

TestDataIndicator を true にするとシミュレーション実行になり、実際の転記は行われません。各 JournalEntryCreateRequest の MessageHeader に <TestDataIndicator>true</TestDataIndicator> を追加することで有効になります。初めて試す際はまず true で動作を確認することをお勧めします。

 

5.1 レスポンスの確認ポイント

正常処理時は HTTP 200 が返り、レスポンス Body に JournalEntryCreateConfirmation が含まれます。以下の項目で成否を確認してください。

レスポンス要素

内容

AccountingDocument

作成された伝票番号（転記失敗時は 0）

CompanyCode

会社コード

Log / MaximumLogItemSeverityCode

最大重要度コード（E=エラー、W=警告、S=成功）

 

5.2 主なエラーコードと対処方法

転記が失敗した場合、Log の Note 要素にエラーメッセージが返ります。よくあるエラーと対処方法を以下に示します。

エラーメッセージ（抜粋）

対処方法

D/C code doesn't match the amount sign (FINS_FI_POSTING_IF 047)

DebitCreditCode と金額の符号を確認。マイナス転記の場合は全明細の符号を揃える。

G/L account & requires a valid tax code (FS 214)

税コード必須の G/L 勘定を使用している。対象勘定のマスターを確認するか税コードを指定する。

For tax code &1, the key date &2 does not fall in any validity period (FF 825)

ヘッダーに <TaxDeterminationDate> を指定して有効期限内の日付を設定する。

Negative postings not allowed (FINS_FI_POSTING_IF056)

会社コードと伝票タイプでマイナス転記が許可されているか Customizing を確認する。

 

本ブログにおいては、認証は Basic 認証（User ID / Password）で行なっています。Postman の場合は Authorization タブで Type を「Basic Auth」に設定し、SAP のユーザー ID とパスワードを入力します。なお、リクエスト内の G/L 勘定科目（GLAccount）・会社コード（CompanyCode）はあらかじめ SAP S/4HANA に登録済みであることが前提です。未登録のマスターデータを指定した場合はエラーレスポンスが返ります。

⚠️  実環境での認証について
本記事では検証の簡便さを優先して Basic 認証を使用していますが、本番環境では以下を推奨します。
・通信路は必ず HTTPS（SSL）で保護する
・可能であれば X.509 クライアント証明書や SAP Assertion Ticket（SSO）を利用する
・呼び出しに使用するユーザーは、必要最小限の権限（FI 転記権限のみ）に絞った専用のシステムユーザーを割り当てる

 

6. 結果を確認する

SOAP リクエストが正常に処理されると、HTTP 200 のレスポンスが返ります。SAP 側では FBL3N（G/L Account Line Item Display）などで仕訳が転記されたことを確認できます。

下図は、SOAP API 経由で作成した仕訳伝票を FBL3N で確認した結果です。G/L Account 66999981 に対して、転記が行われています。

図13: FBL3N で確認した仕訳伝票

 

7. まとめ

本記事では、SAP S/4HANA の SOA Manager を使って SOAP API を公開し、外部クライアントから仕訳伝票を作成するまでの手順を解説しました。手順をまとめると以下のとおりです。

  1. SOAMANAGER（トランザクション）を起動する
  2. Web Service Configuration でサービス定義「JOURNALENTRYCREATEREQUEST CONFI」を検索する
  3. Create Service ウィザードでバインディングを作成する（名前・セキュリティ・プロトコル・オペレーション）
  4. WSDL URL を取得し、SOAP エンドポイント URL を確認する
  5. SOAP クライアントから Basic 認証付きでリクエストを送信する
  6. FBL3N 等で仕訳の転記結果を確認する

 

SAP S/4HANA には REST ベースの OData API も豊富に用意されていますが、既存の EDI やレガシーシステムとの連携など SOAP が必要なシナリオでも、SOA Manager を活用することでスムーズに対応できます。本記事が皆さんの統合プロジェクトの参考になれば幸いです。

 

参考リンク

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub - Journal Entry - Post (Synchronous): https://api.sap.com/api/OP_JOURNALENTRYCREATEREQUESTCONF_IN/overview
  • SAP Help Portal - Journal Entry Create Request Confirmation (Service Description): https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b978f98fc5884ff2aeb10c8fdeb8a43b/f5c8d0579212c525e10000000a4450e5.html

entry.title: Re: OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion Flow with the SAP BTP Destination Service. S/4HANA Cloud.
entry.content:
Hello Piotr,

I have followed the exact steps you mentioned & I can trigger the destination service & even receive a response with destinations. However, I am receiving an error as I am not sending the JWT token header. Could please talk about steps or blogs that you can share, on how to configure the x-user-token to send to the destination service, to eventually receive the access token to be utilized by the client. This will help me complete a scenario which could be heavily utilized.

Error - Retrieval of OAuthToken failed due to: Cannot determine user to propagate for OAuth2SAMLBearerAssertion destination. Either provide user_token JWT token (https://docs.cloudfoundry.org/api/uaa/version/4.7.1/index.html#user-token-grant) when retrieving the destination or configure it with SystemUser.

Thanks,
Rishi
SAP Integration

entry.title: AI Powered i-TS (Intelligent Test Scoper) for Post Upgrade Test of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Releases
entry.content:
Introduction

Every SAP S/4HANA Cloud release brings exciting innovations and continuous business improvements. While these enhancements deliver greater value to customers, they also introduce the need to validate if already available business processes continue to function seamlessly post upgrade to every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Continuous Feature delivery (CFD)

Testing therefore becomes an essential part of every upgrade cycle. However, executing the complete Test Suite for regression test of every SAP S/4HANA Cloud Release and CFD is time and resource intensive. The key challenge is no longer whether to test, but what to test?.

This challenge inspired the development of the Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) (See Figure-1) an application designed to recommend the optimal Post Upgrade Test (PUT) scope for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers.

Figure-1

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud CE 2608 release, we introduce AI-powered i-TS that delivers more efficient yet effective scope for Post Upgrade Tests, thereby further reducing the TCO for out esteemed Customers.

The Journey of Intelligent Test Scoper

Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) are a key offering for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers to validate software upgrades before consumption in productive environments.

The objective is simple:

  • Maintain software quality
  • Reduce effort and upgrade validation time
  • Sustain confidence in Release quality

Traditionally, PUT testing relied on executing even the entire suite of standard scripts delivered (via SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool) . While this approach maximized confidence, it also resulted in significant execution effort and longer upgrade cycles, not to mention higher TCO.

To address this challenge, Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) was introduced to automatically derive an optimized PUT scope based on SAP Standard code changes delivered in every Cloud Release or Continuous Feature Delivery (CFD).

How i-TS Determines the Optimized Scope

The working principle of i-TS can be understood using the following Venn diagram.

Consider the rectangle representing all development objects within an application area.

  • The blue circle represents the objects touched by SAP Standard PUT automates.
  • The yellow circle represents the objects changed in a Cloud Release or CFD.

The overlapping region identifies the automation processes affected by code changes. These processes form the optimized regression scope proposed by Intelligent Test Scoper.

This approach has successfully enabled customers to execute only those automated tests that are technically impacted by a release, reducing unnecessary regression execution.

Why Go Beyond Code Change Analysis?

While code-change analysis marked a major step in the direction of intelligent regression testing, our engineering team continued asking an important question:

Can Artificial Intelligence further reduce the scope proposal while maintaining the same quality standards?

The answer lay in recognizing that code changes alone do not always represent the complete business impact. Several additional factors influence whether a business process should be executed after an upgrade:

  • How frequently customers use the application
  • The overall technical complexity of a test process
  • The relationship between changed and executed development objects
  • Historical regression behaviour observed across previous releases

By learning from these signals collectively, AI has the potential to identify patterns that traditional rule-based approaches cannot.

Building the AI Foundation

As with every successful AI initiative, the journey starts with data. Our objective was not to replace the existing code-change logic, but to enhance it with additional intelligence derived from historical observations.

The AI model was trained using multiple dimensions of information, including:

  • Code Change Information: The optimized scope proposed by the existing i-TS solution based on SAP Standard code changes.
  • Customer Relevancy: Application usage information indicating how frequently customers (cumulatively) execute individual business processes.
  • Process Complexity: The number of ABAP development objects touched by each automated test process across different object types.
  • Change-to-Touch Ratio: The relationship between changed development objects and the total objects touched by an automation process.

Together, these parameters provide a richer representation of the business impact associated with every automated test process.

Training the AI Model

Building confidence in AI recommendations required extensive validation; Instead of relying only on historical accuracy, we simulated real upgrade scenarios by training the model on previous releases and predicting scope for future releases.

 

Training ReleasePrediction Release
CE 2502CE 2408
CE 2502 + CE 2408CE 2508
CE 2502 + CE 2408 + CE 2508CE 2602

This approach closely mirrors the way customers consume SAP S/4HANA Cloud upgrades in production. For every prediction cycle, the AI-generated scope was carefully validated against actual PUT outcomes to ensure there was no quality leakage while measuring optimization gains.

This iterative learning process enabled continuous refinement of the prediction model before introducing it into productive scope recommendations.

The Results

The outcome exceeded our expectations. Earlier releases of Intelligent Test Scoper relied exclusively on SAP Standard code-change analysis and consistently delivered approximately 10% optimization in comparison to the full suite of standard automates.

With of AI-powered scope optimization, i-TS is now able to achieve nearly double the optimization efficiency while continuing to maintain the same quality standards.

Rather than replacing engineering expertise, AI complements the existing code-change analysis by learning from historical release behaviour and customer usage patterns to make more informed scope recommendations.

What's New in CE 2608

Beginning with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud CE 2608 release, Intelligent Test Scoper proposes optimized regression scope using Artificial Intelligence.

The AI-powered scope recommendation combines:

  • SAP Standard code changes
  • Process complexity
  • Change-to-touch ratio
  • Historical learning from previous cloud releases

The result is a smarter regression scope that enables customers to:

  • Execute fewer regression tests without compromising quality
  • Reduce upgrade validation effort
  • Shorten regression execution time
  • Lower testing costs
  • Increase confidence during every SAP S/4HANA Cloud upgrade

Looking Ahead

AI is transforming the way software quality engineering is approached, and Intelligent Test Scoper is an important milestone in that journey.

The introduction of AI-powered scope optimization in CE 2608 demonstrates how historical engineering knowledge, customer usage insights, and machine learning can work together to deliver more intelligent testing recommendations.

As we continue evolving i-TS, our focus remains unchanged: helping SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers perform faster, smarter, and more reliable upgrade testing while maximizing efficiency and maintaining the highest standards of software quality.

We are excited about this journey and look forward to continuously enhance the capabilities to Intelligent Test Scoper in future releases. Refer following Help Portal Documentation to learn more about i-TS (Intelligent test Scoper)

entry.content:
Thanks Daniel for response.  I checked the repository objects imported into system with this and below is my understanding.
1. Only the ABAP objects with ABAP language version 5,2 and X are considered in scope for anakysis.
The enhancements developments ENHO (BAdi , Implicit ) does not have Language version so may be the reason these do not appear in the kernsiefe Fiori app. . 

      

2. The ATC variant ZKNSF_SCORING does not consider the check 'Check for enhancement technology ' is not considered. May be because of this , Implicit enhancement are not reported into ATC scan as Priority 1. 
.
This check is enabled in our ATC variant and it reports the Modifications / Implicit enhancements as Priority error 1 but these are reported into ATC scan jobs executed from kernsiefe Fiori app.

Hi there, 

are you referring to Datasphere (DSP), or to BW-Bridge in DSP? Post some screen shots for easier understanding of the issue. Also are you creating the packages manually or via CLI? 

BW-Bridge -> this is OK. Just re-assign the package

DSP -> Create a transport list in the most used tool - excel :). Have the packages tracked there. Create packages for objects, con is many packages, but pro is that you export/import only the necessary objects without touching anything else. 

Martin

entry.title: “Periodic Supply Protection Maintenance” – The Hidden Champion of Supply Protection
entry.content:
When companies promote a product, they naturally focus on its features and capabilities. Software is no different from a fully automatic coffee machine. Manufacturers highlight how easily you can create perfect milk foam, adjust the coffee strength, or choose from a variety of coffee specialties.

The same applies to Supply Protection in SAP S/4HANA aATP. With every release, new features and innovations are introduced and presented to customers. What often receives less attention, however, are the small utility functions that help keep the solution running smoothly behind the scenes. Yet these tools can be just as important as descaling a coffee machine.

One such hidden champion in Supply Protection is the “Periodic Supply Protection Maintenance” report (RATPSUP_PERIODIC_MAINTENANCE). While it may not be the most prominent feature, it plays a vital role in maintaining system health, data consistency, and overall performance. Over time, the report has evolved and now offers several useful maintenance functions that are worth highlighting.


Image: "Periodic Supply Protection Maintenance" Report

 

Automatic Activation and Deactivation of Supply Protection Objects

When creating a Supply Protection object, you can define a date on which the object should automatically transition from the status “In Planning” to “Activated.” This status change is executed by the Periodic Supply Protection Maintenance report.

The report also supports the automatic deactivation of Supply Protection objects once the current date exceeds the planning horizon end date. At that point, all time buckets have expired and the object no longer serves any operational purpose.

Rather than relying on manual housekeeping activities, the report ensures that object statuses remain aligned with their lifecycle, keeping the Supply Protection landscape clean and up to date.

Deletion of Obsolete Temporary Consumptions

Temporary consumptions are created during availability checks to ensure that quantities cannot be reserved or consumed elsewhere until the temporary consumption is converted into a permanent consumption. This typically happens when a business transaction, such as a sales order, is saved successfully.

As the name suggests, temporary consumptions are intended to exist only for a short period of time. In practice, however, situations may occur where some temporary consumptions are not removed correctly. While the root cause of such inconsistencies should always be analyzed and resolved, the obsolete records themselves can become a problem because they artificially reduce the restricting quantities in Supply Protection.

For this reason, it is highly recommended to regularly delete obsolete temporary consumptions. The maintenance report offers exactly this capability, helping to restore accurate availability calculations and prevent unnecessary supply restrictions.

Deletion of Deactivated Supply Protection Objects

Once a Supply Protection object has been deactivated, it is no longer considered during availability checks. Nevertheless, retaining outdated objects indefinitely is usually not advisable.

Together with their associated permanent consumptions, old Supply Protection objects generate a considerable number of database entries. Over time, this data volume can have an indirect impact on system performance and database efficiency.

At the same time, many organizations prefer to keep recently deactivated objects available for reporting purposes or as templates for creating new Supply Protection configurations. To support this requirement, the report allows you to specify a cutoff date that determines which deactivated Supply Protection objects should be deleted.

This provides a practical balance between retaining useful historical data and keeping the database lean.

Deletion of Unassigned Permanent Consumptions

The latest enhancement to the report will become available with SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS2.

As part of recent improvements to partial delivery handling in Supply Protection, certain consumption records can be created that are required for the calculation of open quantities but are not directly consuming any Supply Protection. These consumptions do not influence availability, restrictions, or protections.

Permanent consumptions that are assigned to a Supply Protection object are automatically deleted when the corresponding Supply Protection object is removed. However, the same does not apply to these unassigned permanent consumptions.

Therefore, the new report functionality enables customers to regularly remove such records and further improve database cleanliness and long-term system efficiency.

Conclusion

Just as regular cleaning and maintenance are essential for ensuring the perfect coffee experience, routine housekeeping activities are equally important for keeping Supply Protection running efficiently.

The Periodic Supply Protection Maintenance report may not be one of the most visible Supply Protection features, but it is a valuable tool for maintaining data quality, preventing unnecessary availability issues, and supporting overall system performance. By automating essential maintenance tasks—from status management to the cleanup of obsolete consumptions and outdated objects—it helps ensure that your Supply Protection setup remains reliable and efficient over time.

Sometimes, the true champions are not the features that attract the most attention, but the tools that quietly keep everything working as intended. The Periodic Supply Protection Maintenance report is exactly such a hidden champion.

Additional Information

SAP Help: Automatic Activation and Deactivation of Supply Protection Objects

SAP Community: Supply Protection in a Nutshell

entry.title: Sign based reclassification based on GL and Segment combination
entry.content:
Hello

I am configuring sign based reclassification with GL as the selection. System is reclassifying by summing up the total of the GL account and apply the rule and reclassify to another FS item. Below is the example.

GL account = X

Total of segment A = -100

Total of segment B = +80

Total of GL X = -20

As the total of GL X is negative, system reclassifies  the entire balance according to the rule. However, our requirement is as below:

Segment A(-100) - Reclassify

Segment B (+80) - Do not reclassify

Could anyone help as to how to manage the above requirment.

Regards,

Rama

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (005) - ABAPシステムのSSL証明書の更新
entry.content:
本記事では、RISE側でのABAPシステムSSL証明書の更新依頼についてご説明させていただきます。
証明書更新に着手される前に、今回登場する証明書関連用語について認識を統一させていただければ幸いです。

証明書関連の用語整理

SSL証明書の更新を進める上で理解しておくべき主要な構成要素について定義いたします。
要素 役割

秘密鍵 (Private Key)暗号化の根幹をなし、厳重な管理が求められる鍵です。
CSR (Certificate Signing Request)秘密鍵を用いて生成されます。これは、認証局（CA）に対して証明書の発行を申請するための「請願書」にあたります。
サーバ証明書 (Signed Certificate)CAがCSRに対して署名を行い、正式に発行された証明書本体です。
中間証明書 (Intermediate Certificate)Root CAとサーバ証明書をつなぎ合わせる橋渡し的な役割を果たします。
Root 証明書 (Root Certificate)CA自身が自己署名した、信頼の基盤となる証明書です。

 

SSL証明書の更新依頼について

SSL証明書の更新は、下記の専用SRテンプレートを通じてご依頼いただけます。
ご利用推奨SRテンプレート: Manage SSL Certificates for SAP Applications and HANA DB

この専用SRテンプレートを用いることで、ABAPシステムだけでなく、Web DispatcherやSCC（SAP Cloud Connector）、HANA DBなどを含む広範囲の証明書更新案件に対応可能です。
現状、SR上でご選択いただける主なオプションは以下の5通りです。


1.新規証明書の作成 (Create Certificate)

SRの情報に基づき、RISE側でCSRを生成し、お客様へ提供いたします。その後、お客様がCAに申請を行い、発行された各種証明書（サーバ、Root、中間）をRISE側にご提示いただくことで、証明書更新作業が依頼できます。

ご注意点:
RISE側にて秘密鍵を保有しているため、PFXファイル作成などで秘密鍵が必要となる場合は、必ずSRにてRISE側の担当者へご依頼いただくか、RISE側の担当者にメールでご連絡をお願いいたします。

通常の作業フロー:

  1. お客様にて本オプションでSRを起票し、CSR作成に必要な情報をご提供ください。
  2. RISEにてCSRを作成し、お客様へ提供いたします。（秘密鍵はRISE側で保有しております）
  3. お客様にてCSRをCAに提出し、証明書発行をご依頼ください。
  4. CAから発行された各種証明書をRISE側にご提供ください。
  5. 当社にてSSL証明書更新作業を実施いたします。
  • ワイルドカード証明書をご利用の場合：
    開発、検証、本番環境などで同一のワイルドカード証明書をご利用の場合、CSR作成およびCAへの発行依頼は開発環境のみで実施すれば問題ございません。検証・本番環境では、開発環境で作成した証明書群（Root, 中間, サーバ証明書および秘密鍵）を利用します。
  • シングルドメイン証明書をご利用の場合：
    環境ごとにCSR作成とCAへの発行依頼を実施していただく必要がございます。

2.証明書の有効期限延長 (Extend Certificate Validity)

オプション1(Create Certificate)と手続きはほぼ同様ですが、既存のPSEファイルからCSRが生成される点が異なります。現在運用中のSSL証明書を延長する場合は、本オプションをご利用いただけます。

3.署名済み証明書によるリニューアル (Renew with Attached Certificate)

お客様側ですでにPFXファイル（pkcs#12）をお持ちの場合に最適なオプションです。

  • CA側にCSR作成およびPFXファイルの提供までご依頼いただける場合は、CA側にて対応をお願いいたします。
  • CA側の対応が難しい場合は、お客様側でCSRを作成し、発行済みの証明書を受領後にPFXファイルとしてまとめることも可能です。

本オプションのメリット:
このフローでは、基本的にSR上のやり取りが簡潔で済み、お客様側のSR起票をもって手続きが完了します。

PFXファイル（pkcs#12）について
PFXファイルは、複数の証明書関連ファイルをパスワード付きの形で一つにまとめたものと考えてください。RISE側へご提供いただく際は、以下のファイルを含めていただくことを推奨いたします。

  1. Root証明書
  2. 中間証明書 (必須ではありませんが、CAから提供された場合はご同封ください)
  3. サーバ証明書
  4. 秘密鍵

また、PFXファイルのパスワードは「Customer Remote Logon Depot」に記載をお願いいたします。詳細については、下記のSAP Notesをご参照ください。
https://me.sap.com/notes/1773689

4.参照システムからのコピー (Copy from Reference System)

こちらはワイルドカード証明書をご利用のお客様向けのオプションです。
開発・検証・本番環境で同一のワイルドカード証明書をご利用の場合、先に開発環境での更新作業が完了した後、検証環境や本番環境の証明書更新を依頼する際に、開発環境を参照システムとして利用していただくことになります。

現在ご利用中の証明書がワイルドカード型か、シングルドメイン型かをご確認いただければ幸いです。

証明書の種類によるカバー範囲の違い

種類 カバー範囲 例

シングルドメイン1つのFQDNのみをカバーします。www.example.com
ワイルドカード同一ドメイン内の全てのサブドメインをカバーします。*.example.com

 

5.証明書リストへの追加（トラスト登録） (Add Certificate to Certificate List)

特定のPSEの証明書トラストリストに証明書を追加したい場合に利用するオプションです。お客様の業務要件に応じて、SAPSSLS/SAPSSLC/SAPSSLAなどの証明書リストへ追加作業を行います。
下記でよくご利用されるシナリオをご紹介しますが、多くの場合、オプション3（Renew with Attached Certificate）をご利用いただくことで最もシンプルに依頼が可能です。

下記で良く利用するシナリオをご紹介します。なお、シナリオ1とシナリオ2は、オプション3(Renew with Attached Certificate)を利用して下記のようにシンプルに依頼する事が可能です。

Task Required: Renew with Attached Certificate
Certificate Name: SAPSSLS
Upload Certificate: CAから受領、または作成されたPFXファイルをアップロードしてください。
Confirm credentials maintained in Customer Remote Logon Depot if certificate is password protected: Yes

シナリオ1 - ワイルドカード証明書の更新

 下記は、S/4HANA(開発/検証/本番)のABAPシステムのSAPSSLSの証明書更新を例にしております。
基本的な流れは下記となります。

No. 対象環境 作業担当者 作業内容 必要なファイル 補足

1開発環境　　お客様　オプション2を利用してSRを作成‐ 
2 RISECSRを作成し、お客様に提供CSRファイル　　秘密鍵はRISE側で保有している
3 お客様CSRを利用してCAへ証明書の署名を依頼Root証明書
中間証明書
サーバ証明書　　		 
4 お客様No3でCAから受領したファイルをRISE側に提供Root証明書
中間証明書
サーバ証明書		 
5 RISE証明書の更新作業を実施‐ 
6 お客様証明書更新作業結果を確認し、問題なければSRのステータスをCloseに変更する‐ 
7検証環境お客様オプション4を利用してSRを作成‐ 
8 RISE証明書の更新作業を実施‐ 
9 お客様証明書更新作業結果を確認し、問題なければSRのステータスをCloseに変更する‐ 
10本番環境お客様オプション4を利用してSRを作成‐ 
11 RISE証明書の更新作業を実施‐ 
12 お客様証明書更新作業結果を確認し、問題なければSRのステータスをCloseに変更する‐ 

No.1でのSR作成時のサンプルとなります。

 


開発環境でのSSL証明書の更新作業が完了し、お客様側での動作確認が完了後、オプション4を利用して検証環境(No.7)と本番環境(No.10)でのSSL証明書更新を依頼してください。

 Task Required: Renew with Attached Certificate
Certificate Name: SAPSSLS
Reference System: 開発環境ABAPシステムのSIDを指定してください。

シナリオ2 - シングルドメイン証明書の更新

シングルドメイン証明書の場合、開発/検証/本番環境ごとに下記のように実施する必要がございます。

No. 作業担当者 作業内容 必要なファイル 補足

1お客様　オプション2を利用してSRを作成‐ 
2RISECSRを作成し、お客様に提供CSRファイル　　秘密鍵はRISE側で保有している
3お客様CSRを利用してCAへ証明書の署名を依頼Root証明書
中間証明書
サーバ証明書		 
4お客様No3でCAから受領したファイルをRISE側に提供Root証明書
中間証明書
サーバ証明書		 
5RISE証明書の更新作業を実施‐ 
6お客様証明書更新作業結果を確認し、問題なければSRのステータスをCloseに変更する‐ 

 

シナリオ3 - Certificate Listへの証明書追加

お客様側の業務要件に応じて、SAPSSLS/SAPSSLCなどへ証明書追加が必要な場合、ご利用願います。

 Task Required: Add Certificate to Certificate List
Certificate Name: お客様の業務要件に応じて、SAPSSLS、SAPSSLCなどを指定してください。複数指定可能です。
Upload Certificate: Certificate Listへ追加したい証明書をアップロードしてください。

まとめ

ご利用の証明書の種類を確認し、SSL証明書更新作業を計画していただけますようにお願いします。
また、SSL証明書の更新に多くのオプションが用意されておりますが、オプション3(Renew with Attached Certificate)が最もシンプルとなります。

entry.title: Talk to Your Localized Reports: How Joule Runs A/P, A/R, and Stock Reports in Native Language
entry.content:
Running a country-specific aging report used to mean remembering the transaction code, the exact parameter names, and the local compliance rules that shape every field. Now you can just ask.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces AI-assisted localized business data management — a new capability that lets Joule, SAP's generative AI digital assistant, run classic localized reports through natural-language prompts while respecting every country- and region-specific requirement.

Scope Note

This blog post covers SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and the new AI Case Manage Business Data Using Localized Classic Applications. Activation requires the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG and the corresponding business catalog per report. Three localized reports are currently in scope: the A/P Aging Report, the A/R Aging Report, and the Stock Overview Report. The reports were originally built for specific countries, but any customer can adopt them wherever they see a fit — simply copy the delivered business catalog into your own business role and assign it to the target users. Other editions (on-premise, Cloud Private Edition) and other localized reports are not covered here.

Main Insight

Localized reports are where compliance meets complexity. Every country encodes its own rules for aging buckets, currency handling, cleared-item treatment, and inventory posting — and each rule shows up as another parameter on a classic report screen.

The result: teams spend more time configuring the report than reading it. New users take weeks to get productive. And every parameter mis-entry is a potential compliance gap.

AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management flips that. Joule sits on top of the same classic reports your finance and supply-chain teams already use — but it understands the country-specific context. You describe what you need in plain English. Joule fills the parameters, respects the localization rules, and runs the report.

You keep the compliance-certified backend. You lose the friction.

Three Reports, One Conversational Interface

Three classic localized reports ship with the initial release. Each requires its own business catalog assigned to the user.

1. Accounts Payable (A/P) Aging Report

  • Report ID: RFIDCN_AP_AGING
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by company code, supplier, and key date · select item types · configure due-date buckets and currency · analyze amounts in transaction or reporting currency
  • Example prompt: "Run the A/P Aging Report for specific suppliers or supplier ranges within a company."

2. Accounts Receivable (A/R) Aging Report

  • Report ID: RFIDCN_AR_AGING
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAR_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by company code, customer, and key date · include or exclude cleared and special G/L items · define due-date buckets and reporting currency
  • Example prompt: "Run the A/R Aging Report for customers S13100251 to S13100253 in company 1310."

3. Stock Overview Report

  • Report ID: J_3RMOBVEDH
  • Business Catalog: SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_MOBVED_PC
  • What Joule handles: filter by plant and product number · select a posting date or date range · filter by storage location and batch · review inventory status in context
  • Example prompt: "Run the Stock Overview Report for product tg-d010_ua in plant 6510 with posting date today."

Same reports. Same compliance guarantees. New way to reach them.

Not restricted to the original country. These reports ship as SAP-delivered business catalogs, but you don't have to run them only in the country they were originally designed for. Copy the delivered catalog into a custom business role and assign the role to any user or business unit that needs the same reporting logic. For step-by-step guidance, see Copying and Editing Business Roles — SAP Help Portal.

Activation in 6 Steps

The capability is off by default. Enable it with the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG:

  1. Open your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition configuration environment with an administrator user that has the required authorization.
  2. Search for and open the configuration activity Activate Features.
  3. In the feature list, search for the feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG.
  4. Review the feature details to confirm you are activating the AI-assisted localized business data management capability.
  5. Choose the activation option and confirm when prompted.
  6. Wait until the system confirms successful activation, then assign the required business catalog(s) to your users.

Once activated, users can invoke Joule from any Joule-enabled entry point in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and prompt the localized reports directly.

Practical Takeaway

Start with one report and one country. Pick the localized report your team runs most often — A/P Aging for a China entity is a good candidate — and follow this path:

  • Verify your system runs SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with Joule enabled
  • Activate feature toggle GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG in a test tenant
  • Assign the matching business catalog (e.g. SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC) to a pilot user
  • Ask Joule to run the report using one of the example prompts above
  • Compare Joule's parameter selections to what your team would have entered manually
  • Roll out to the full team once you confirm the compliance rules and outputs match

You'll know it's working when your finance team stops asking "which parameter goes where?" and starts asking "can I get the same report for last month?" — because now they can.

Summary

Localized reports carry the weight of country-specific compliance, and that complexity usually lives in the report screen. AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management moves the complexity into Joule — users describe what they need in natural language, and the classic report runs with the correct localized parameters. Three reports ship first (A/P Aging, A/R Aging, Stock Overview), with more expected as the capability expands. Because the reports are delivered as SAP business catalogs, you can copy them into your own business roles and reuse them wherever the reporting logic fits your process — not only in the country they were originally built for.

Key Takeaways

  • Compliance stays in the backend. Joule doesn't reinterpret local rules — it drives the existing compliance-certified classic reports, so audit and regulatory guarantees remain intact.
  • Activation is a single feature toggle. GLO_DAS_LOCLZN_CLSICAPPL_INTEG in the Activate Features configuration activity turns the capability on. No custom development required.
  • Business catalog assignment is per report. Each of the three initial reports has its own catalog (SAP_GLO_BC_CLSICAPP_IDCNAP_PC, _IDCNAR_PC, _MOBVED_PC) — assign only what each user needs.
  • Productivity gain is immediate. New users skip the parameter-memorization phase. Experienced users skip the parameter-entry step. Both groups reach the same compliant result faster.
  • Scope is intentionally narrow at launch. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition only, three reports only. Plan pilots around what's shipped today, and monitor SAP Help Portal for the expanding scope.
  • Reuse beyond the original country. Copy the delivered business catalogs into custom business roles and assign them wherever the reporting logic fits — the reports aren't locked to the country they were originally designed for.

More Information

  • AI-Assisted Localized Business Data Management — SAP Help Portal
  • Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition — SAP Help Portal
  • Activating Business AI and Assigning Users — SAP Help Portal
  • Copying and Editing Business Roles — SAP Help Portal
